scope: finale scaffold code in trajectories/rl-offpolicy-continuous/04-crossq-answer.md (Actor, BatchRenorm1d, QNetwork, OffPolicyAlgorithm)
focus: code-vs-reference correctness: faithful CrossQ (Bhatt, Palenicek et al., ICLR 2024, arXiv:1902.05605) re-expressed in the task's fixed 256-wide scaffold
reference: ICLR 2024 paper Figure 2 / CrossQ description and the official adityab/CrossQ repo
evidence: No correctness bugs found. BatchRenorm1d implements detached clipped r/d corrections, running-stat momentum updates, and train/eval branches correctly. _q_forward_cross concatenates (s,a) and (s',a'), runs one critic forward, then splits (CrossQ's core target-network-replacing move). No target Q networks or Polyak updates anywhere; next-state value detached. TD target uses min(q1_next,q2_next) - alpha*next_log_pi. Critics in train() for the joint critic update and eval() for the actor Q-evaluation. SAC tanh-Gaussian actor with rsample, squash log-prob correction, automatic entropy tuning to target_entropy=-action_dim. The expected 256-wide critics (vs paper 2048) and policy_frequency=1 were not flagged, as instructed.
reason: Finale code-correctness gate for the rl-offpolicy-continuous trajectory; methods/crossq derivation trace reused as the reference.
